title: Warm Sichuan Noodles Dumplings
description: Earthy, crispy, quick-fix Sichuan dim sum featuring egg noodles tossed with spicy dumplings.
instructions: Cook egg noodles in a pot of salted boiling water until al dente, about 4 minutes, then drain and set aside.

In a large bowl, combine ground pork, shredded cabbage, minced garlic, minced ginger, beaten egg, soy sauce, sesame oil, chili oil, Sichuan peppercorn powder, and chopped scallions; mix until ingredients are well incorporated.
Place a dumpling wrapper in the palm of your hand and spoon about 1 tablespoon of the pork mixture into the center.
Fold the wrapper in half and pinch the edges tightly to seal, pressing gently to create a pleated dim sum style.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat; once hot, add the dumplings and sear for 1 minute until bottoms are golden brown.
Carefully pour 1 cup of hot water over the dumplings, cover the skillet, and let them steam for 5 minutes.
Remove the lid, drain any remaining water, and flip the dumplings to crisp the other side for an additional 1 minute.
Transfer the dumplings to a serving dish, toss the cooked noodles with a splash of sesame oil, chili oil, and a pinch of Sichuan peppercorn powder, then combine with the dumplings before serving.
